title: Capitanopsis angustifolia
taxonomy: Capitanopsis angustifolia is a flowering plant, and it belongs to the family Ericaceae. This family includes many other flowering plants, commonly known as heath or heather. The Ericaceae family is part of the order Ericales, which consists of various flowering plants. The Ericales order is further classified under the class Magnoliopsida, in the kingdom Plantae. The classification of Capitanopsis angustifolia reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants within these taxonomic groups.

intro: Capitanopsis angustifolia, also known as Mexican chamomile or narrow-leaved capitanopsis, is a flowering plant native to Mexico, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including anxiety, insomnia, and inflammation, due to its potential health benefits, which include reducing stress and promoting relaxation. The bioactive constituents of Capitanopsis angustifolia include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
